steps.title: Identify the structure around the blank
steps.content: The sentence before the blank is a complete independent clause: “The sand at Talofofo Beach in Guam is an ______.” After the blank comes “sand gets its shade from deposits of gray- and tan-hued quartz and feldspar”—another independent clause. The word “though” signals a contrast: most sand is cream or beige, but Talofofo Beach's sand is different. The punctuation at the blank must (1) end the first independent clause and (2) introduce the contrasting explanation.

steps.title: Eliminate choices that mishandle the punctuation, then confirm the match
steps.content:
• "exception, though most": A comma alone cannot join two independent clauses—this creates a comma splice. ✗
  • "exception: though most": The colon correctly ends the first independent clause and introduces the elaborating contrast. “Though most sand gets its shade from quartz and feldspar” explains why Talofofo Beach is an exception. ✓
  • "exception, though, most": The extra comma after “though” breaks the syntax; the clause that follows becomes ungrammatical. ✗
  • "exception, though. Most": Ends the sentence after “though,” leaving the word “though” dangling with nothing to contrast. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
